pagoda_stack.description: Tiered Chinese pagoda or temple tower assembled from repeated octagonal tiers, eaves, spire, and plaza details.
checks: main body should remain octagonal, not square
roof/eave blocks should appear on every tier
glass ratio should stay low or only appear as small windows
pagoda_stack.scale_guidance: For landmark pagodas, increase height by adding tiers first, then widen the base. Do not stretch one tier vertically into a plain tower.

temple_hall.description: Traditional Chinese or Jiangnan hall with platform, columns, timber frame, raised roof, ridge ornaments, and courtyard.
checks: roof should dominate the silhouette
columns should read clearly on the front facade
avoid large curtain-wall glass
temple_hall.scale_guidance: Scale width by increasing bays. Scale depth by adding side halls or courtyard walls.

jiangnan_water_town.description: White-wall black-tile canal-side buildings with stone paths, small bridges, wood windows, and compact massing.

component_sequence.repeat: place across canal or entry path

checks: building height should stay modest
roofs should be dark and pitched
bridge should be stone arch, not cable bridge
jiangnan_water_town.scale_guidance: Grow as a street/canal ensemble, not as one tall tower.

modern_glass_gate.description: Large modern landmark with two towers, high void, skybridge, curtain wall, offices, and internal lighting.
primitive_needs: two leg towers with office floors
checks: central void must remain clear
left/right legs need usable office floors
glass ratio should be high
lighting should be repeated on office floors
modern_glass_gate.scale_guidance: Use actual height/width/depth proportions. If the target is not a gate-shaped landmark, use office_tower instead.

office_tower.description: Generic modern office tower with curtain wall, structural core, repeated floors, lobby, services, and lights.
checks: facade should show repeated floors
interior floors should not be hollow empty shells
avoid ancient roof components unless prompt asks for fusion style
office_tower.scale_guidance: Scale height by floor count and keep each floor readable.

stone_arch_bridge.description: Historic stone arch bridge with masonry abutments, arched side profile, deck, rails, lamps, and optional water.
checks: arch void must be visible
deck should be walkable
keep material mostly stone
stone_arch_bridge.scale_guidance: Scale by span and arch height. For very long bridges, repeat arches rather than stretching one arch.

suspension_bridge.description: Modern suspension or cable bridge with towers, deck, cables, hangers, rails, road markings, and lights.

component_sequence.repeat: repeat or scale along bridge length

checks: cable line should be above the deck
hangers should connect cable to deck rhythmically
avoid stone arch massing
suspension_bridge.scale_guidance: Scale length by repeating segments; increase tower height with span.

twisted_lattice_tower.description: Canton Tower / Xiaomanyao style hyperboloid observation tower with narrow waist, rotating lattice, observation decks, and antenna.
primitive_needs: wide base tapering to a narrow waist and widening again near top
twisted diagonal lattice around the outside
red or white night lighting points
checks: height should be at least 2.5x the max body width
waist radius should be much smaller than base radius
silhouette should be curved/twisted, not stacked tiers
do not add ancient eaves, pagoda roofs, or gold spires
twisted_lattice_tower.scale_guidance: For Canton Tower-like builds, preserve height first. Use 180-260 blocks tall, 50-80 blocks wide, and make the waist around 45-60% of the height.
